name: Regression/exception-handling
description: Server error-handling conformance (from ExceptionTests.cs): unknown routes return 404, and unsupported HTTP methods against a resource endpoint return 405. Two source tests requiring in-process throwing middleware aren't portable to an external server and are skipped.

test.name: Unknown route returns 404
test.description: GET a path that matches no FHIR resource type or interaction; server must return 404 (OperationOutcome shape checks are warningOnly since generic routing 404s may not include a FHIR body).

operation.description: GET a path outside the FHIR interaction model

assert.description: Server must return HTTP 404 Not Found
Response body should be an OperationOutcome (warningOnly: routing-level 404s may bypass FHIR error formatting)
OperationOutcome should contain an error-severity issue (warningOnly, see above)
Issue code should be 'not-found' (warningOnly: exact code for routing-level errors varies by server)

test.name: Unsupported HTTP method against a resource endpoint (approximated)
test.description: HEAD to Patient (approximating the source test's HEAD against a fixed static route, which doesn't exist on external servers); either 405 Method Not Allowed or 200 (HEAD aliased to GET) is acceptable, so both are warningOnly.

assert.description: Alternative A: server rejects HEAD with 405 Method Not Allowed (informational: not a FHIR spec requirement)
Alternative B: server aliases HEAD to GET and returns 200 (informational: also acceptable)
